message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should return sum of base premiums for two different items"
Expected behavior: generateQuote([{type: "sword"}, {type: "amulet"}], customer) returns object with basePremium of 160
Current error: Expected 160, Received 100 (only first item is summed, second ignored)
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: Let me read the current test and implementation files.
The issue is clear. The current implementation only looks at `items[0].type` instead of summing all items. The minimal fix is to sum across all items.

input.new_string:
const basePremium = items.reduce((sum, item) => sum + BASE_PREMIUMS[item.type], 0);
  return { basePremium };

content.content: The file /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_07-09-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6/src/claim-office.ts has been updated successfully.

input.description: Run tests to verify all pass

content.text:
All 6 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Changed `generateQuote` to sum base premiums across all items using `reduce` instead of only reading `items[0].type`.
**Result**: All tests now pass (6 passing, 20 todo)
**Approach**: This is minimal - the previous single-item lookup was forced to generalize by this test demanding multi-item summation. The `reduce` is the simplest expression of summing all items.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_07-09-01_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_sonnet-4-6/src/claim-office.ts`
